hardware.name: NVIDIA DGX Spark (GB10 Grace Blackwell)
hardware.memory_topology: unified — same 128 GB pool serves CPU and GPU; allocations from one shrink the budget of the other; OOM hangs the whole box (see HANDOFF.md 2026-04-22 incident)
hardware.interconnect_to_other_gpus: none — single-node only; multi-GPU requires the cloud envelope (see Looking Beyond Spark series)

memory_budget_rules_of_thumb.training_overhead_multiplier: ~4× params for full fine-tune (params + grads + optimizer state + activations); ~1.5× for LoRA
memory_budget_rules_of_thumb.kv_cache_per_token_per_layer: 2 × hidden_size × bytes_per_dtype (one for K, one for V); see kv-cache-arithmetic-at-inference article for the canonical walkthrough

practical_inference_envelope.8B params bf16: fits with room — ~16 GB weights + KV; 24.8 tok/s measured on NIM
practical_inference_envelope.70B params bf16: marginal — 140 GB weights alone exceeds the unified pool; requires fp8/int4 quant or offload
practical_inference_envelope.70B params fp8: ~70 GB weights; leaves ~50 GB for KV + activations + system; tight but possible
practical_inference_envelope.405B+ params: out of envelope at any common quant; belongs in Looking Beyond Spark

practical_finetune_envelope.LoRA on 8B: trivially fits; demonstrated across multiple Foundations + Fine-tuning articles
practical_finetune_envelope.LoRA on 70B fp8: borderline; requires careful batch + grad_accum tuning
practical_finetune_envelope.Full fine-tune on 8B: fits with bf16 + grad checkpointing + small batch

nim.label: NVIDIA NIM (NVIDIA Inference Microservices)
nim.purpose: Containerized inference microservices for popular open weights with TensorRT-LLM optimization built in
known_limits: 8192-token context window default; chunking math must respect it (see project-spark-nim-context-window memory)
NIM_GPU_MEM_FRACTION caps the KV reservation; 0.5 default leaves headroom for the OS
Cold start can take minutes when pulling weights

nemo.label: NVIDIA NeMo Framework
nemo.purpose: Training, fine-tuning, distillation, curation across LLM/multimodal/speech
known_limits: /opt/venv pip trap on NeMo containers — installs go to /usr/local/lib unless you use /opt/venv/bin/python3 -m pip (see feedback memory)
Multi-node distributed training APIs assume a cluster; on Spark you're capped at single-node configs

tensorrt_llm.label: NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM
tensorrt_llm.purpose: Compiled inference engines with paged KV cache, fp8/int4 kernels, in-flight batching
known_limits: Engine builds are model + dtype + max-context-length specific; rebuilding for new shapes is slow
fp8 quant requires Hopper+ (Blackwell on Spark satisfies)
Use `--use_paged_context_fmha` and `--use_fp8_context_fmha` for KV efficiency on Spark

triton.label: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server
triton.purpose: Multi-model serving with batching, ensembles, custom backends
known_limits: Setup complexity higher than NIM for single-model use cases

nemo_retriever.label: NVIDIA NeMo Retriever (NIM-served embed + rerank)
nemo_retriever.purpose: Embedding + reranker microservices tuned for retrieval pipelines
known_limits: 1B reranker is the practical default on Spark; larger rerankers cut into LLM headroom
pgvector retrieval ~70 ms top-5; reranker ~40 ms per call (measured)

pgvector.label: pgvector (Postgres extension)
pgvector.purpose: Vector search inside Postgres; the project's default RAG store
known_limits: IVFFlat / HNSW tradeoffs documented; HNSW preferred for low-latency

nemoclaw.label: NVIDIA NemoClaw (OpenClaw-in-OpenShell sandboxed agents)
nemoclaw.purpose: Local sandboxed agent stack with Nemotron backing for tool-using agents
known_limits: Pinned to Nemotron at ~22 tok/s baseline
k3s/CoreDNS quirks on Ubuntu 24.04 cgroup v2 (see nemoclaw-guru skill)

nemo_guardrails.label: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
nemo_guardrails.purpose: Programmable safety + topical rails around LLM responses

openclaw.label: OpenClaw CLI (Ollama-integrated)
openclaw.purpose: Ollama-backed agent CLI for lightweight tool use
known_limits: Ollama models stack — second concurrent model can OOM the box

ollama.label: Ollama
ollama.purpose: Local LLM runner; useful for quick model swaps but not the production path
known_limits: Multi-model loading exceeds 128 GB unified pool — hard hang risk (see project-spark-unified-memory-oom memory)

out_of_envelope_signals: Pretraining anything ≥ 1B from scratch (need a cluster, see Looking Beyond Spark)
Multi-node distributed training of any size
Mixture-of-Experts at large total-param counts paired with long context
Anything requiring B200 / H200 / multiple H100s simultaneously
Training datasets that exceed local NVMe budget
in_envelope_signals: Fine-tuning open-weight LLMs ≤ 70B with LoRA / QLoRA
Inference-time techniques: speculative decoding, prompt caching, structured generation, constrained decoding
RAG pipelines: chunking, embedding, retrieval, reranking, fusion, hybrid search
Agentic systems: tool use, multi-step planning, sandboxed execution
Distillation from a frontier model into a small student
Quantization studies (fp8, int4, AWQ, GPTQ) on existing weights
Long-context inference economics (KV cache, paged attention, attention sinks)
Observability and instrumentation of inference and agent traces

stage_routing_hints.foundations: papers about install / setup / hardware / drivers / day-one onboarding
stage_routing_hints.training: pretraining or continued pretraining studies; loss/loss-curve dynamics
stage_routing_hints.inference: serving, throughput, latency, KV cache, paged attention, quant
stage_routing_hints.agentic: tool use, multi-agent, sandboxes, planning, code execution
stage_routing_hints.observability: tracing, profiling, eval pipelines, judge models, telemetry
stage_routing_hints.dev-tools: Nsight, CUDA tooling, IDE integrations, debug workflows

series_routing_hints.Foundations: shared install / setup substrate; cross-cutting onboarding
series_routing_hints.Second Brain: RAG over the user's own corpus; query-time generation
series_routing_hints.LLM Wiki: compile-time synthesis; LLM maintains a linted knowledge base
series_routing_hints.Autoresearch: autonomous experimentation; agent runs overnight loops, edits, measures, decides
series_routing_hints.Looking Beyond Spark: arithmetic that extrapolates beyond the 128 GB envelope to H100/H200/B200/SuperPOD
series_routing_hints.Frontier Scout: the meta-series — papers + the system that found them
